name: Nutrition
description: Vitamins, macronutrients, MyPlate, and the food-science basics behind a balanced diet.

questions.q: Which month is National Nutrition Month in the United States?

source.notes: Promoted by the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics.

questions.q: What are the three major macronutrients?

source.original_a: Lean protein, whole grains, fruits, vegetables
source.notes: Macronutrients are needed in large amounts; vitamins and minerals are micronutrients.

questions.q: Which vitamin's deficiency causes scurvy, the disease historically common among sailors?

source.notes: Citrus fruits are the classic remedy — hence British sailors' nickname 'limeys.'

questions.q: What vitamin does the human body produce when skin is exposed to sunlight?

source.notes: Triggered by UVB exposure on cholesterol in the skin.

questions.q: Which vitamin's deficiency causes the bone-softening disease rickets, especially in children?

source.notes: Why milk is fortified with vitamin D in many countries.

questions.q: How many calories are in 1 gram of fat?

source.notes: Protein and carbohydrates each provide 4 calories per gram; alcohol 7.

questions.q: How many calories are in 1 gram of protein or carbohydrate?
What U.S. nutrition guideline replaced the food pyramid in 2011?

source.notes: Introduced by the USDA under Michelle Obama's 'Let's Move' initiative.

questions.q: What roughly half of MyPlate's recommended plate should consist of?

source.original_a: Half fruits and vegetables, half grains and lean protein, fat-free or low-fat milk or yogurt
source.notes: The other half is split between grains and protein.

questions.q: Approximately what percentage of dietary sodium in the U.S. comes from packaged and restaurant foods (rather than the salt shaker)?

source.notes: Per CDC. Why simply 'cooking with less salt' isn't enough.

questions.q: What is the chemical name for table salt?

source.notes: NaCl. About 40% sodium and 60% chloride by mass.

questions.q: Which nutrient is the body's primary source of energy?

source.notes: Specifically glucose, derived from carbohydrate digestion.

questions.q: Approximately how many minutes of moderate aerobic activity per week do U.S. guidelines recommend for adults?

source.original_a: At least two hours and 30 minutes
source.notes: 150 minutes (= 2 hours 30 minutes) per week, per CDC.

questions.q: How many minutes of physical activity per day are recommended for children and teens?
Which type of dietary fiber dissolves in water and helps lower blood cholesterol?

source.notes: Found in oats, beans, apples, and citrus.

questions.q: Which mineral is found in dairy products and is essential for strong bones?

source.notes: Vitamin D is required for calcium absorption.

questions.q: Which mineral is most commonly associated with red blood cell production?

source.notes: Iron deficiency is the most common nutritional deficiency worldwide.

questions.q: Which diet, named after a region of the world, emphasizes olive oil, fish, vegetables, and whole grains?

source.notes: Frequently cited as one of the healthiest dietary patterns.

questions.q: Which low-carbohydrate diet, popular since the 1970s and again in the 2000s, is named after its creator, a cardiologist?
Which essential amino acid must be obtained from the diet (humans can't synthesize it)?

source.notes: There are 9 essential amino acids — Lysine is one of them. Alanine, glycine, and glutamic acid are non-essential.
